theme: adding express tunnels so every courier reaches the depot fast
title: Express Tunnels to the Depot
seed_problem: add k shortcut edges to minimize the worst-case random-walk hitting time to a marked depot
mutation: composite: the objective is scored via the exact commute-time identity C(u,t)=2*m*R(u,t) (mass times effective resistance), and candidate shortcuts are restricted to a fixed input list containing planted 'tied-pair' arm structures -- so choosing which k of the M candidates to add is a combinatorial resistance-vs-mass-dilution trade-off, not a single textbook shortest-path/MST/max-flow computation.
strategies: trivial: add zero shortcuts (reproduces the checker's do-nothing baseline exactly)

naive greedy: repeatedly locate the single currently-farthest vertex (BFS-distance proxy for hitting time) and wire it to whatever nearby candidate shortens ITS distance the most, always spending the full budget k

insight (strong): maintain the exact effective-resistance matrix via a Sherman-Morrison rank-1 update per candidate, greedily add only the shortcut that most reduces the TRUE global worst-case C(u,t) = 2*m*R(u,t), and STOP once no remaining candidate beats the mass it would add -- this favors 'tied-pair' cross-links (which cut two symmetric bottleneck resistances in half for one unit of mass) over the deceptively appealing single 'direct-to-depot' shortcuts that fix one courier but barely move the global worst case
